seo.title: Indiana Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Indiana — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license indiana, dental assistant certification indiana, RDA indiana, DANB CDA indiana, dental assistant radiography indiana

board.name: Indiana Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: Indiana Professional Licensing Agency, Attn: State Board of Dentistry, 402 West Washington Street, Room W072, Indianapolis, IN 46204

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Indiana.

steps.title: Understand Indiana's Regulations
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the Indiana State Board of Dentistry's regulations. Note that a general Dental Assistant license is not required, but specific certifications are mandated for Expanded Functions Dental Assistants (EFDA) and Dental Radiography.

steps.title: Identify Required Certifications
steps.description: Determine if your intended duties require specific certifications, such as the Expanded Functions Dental Assistant (EFDA) Certificate or the Dental Radiography Certificate. Review the eligibility criteria for each on the Board of Dentistry's website.

steps.title: Create a MyLicense.IN.gov Account
steps.description: Visit the MyLicense.IN.gov portal, select 'Register a Person Account,' and follow the instructions to set up your personal user ID and password, which will be used for all online applications.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application for Certification
steps.description: Log into your MyLicense.IN.gov account, select the specific application for your desired certification (e.g., 'Expanded Functions Dental Assistant Certificate' or 'Dental Radiography Certificate'), and accurately complete all required sections, including uploading any necessary supporting documents like transcripts or national certification proof.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee for your chosen certification through the MyLicense.IN.gov portal using a credit card or electronic check. (Note: These fees are for certifications, as no general DA license exists).

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application's status by logging into your MyLicense.IN.gov account and promptly respond to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Indiana State Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Indiana does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Indiana require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Indiana does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Indiana?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Indiana.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Indiana?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Indiana?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Indiana. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Indiana does not license general dental assistants. Radiography certification required for x-rays.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Indiana
hero.dek: In Indiana, a general Dental Assistant license is not required. However, individuals performing Expanded Functions or Dental Radiography must obtain specific certifications through the MyLicense.IN.gov online portal.

variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit MyLicense.IN.gov, click 'Register a Person Account', and follow the prompts to create a user ID and password. Once registered, log in to select the appropriate application for certifications like Expanded Functions Dental Assistant or Dental Radiography.
